title: Do not default to centered modals for every overlay interaction
summary: AI-generated interfaces default to a centered modal card with dark backdrop for every overlay interaction — confirmations, details, forms, settings — regardless of whether context preservation would be better served by a side panel, inline expansion, or toast.
instruction: Choose the overlay format based on the user's task, not the modal default. Use side panels for detail views where context with the parent page matters, inline expansions for lightweight reveals, and toasts for transient confirmations. Reserve centeral modals for confirmations and flows requiring exclusive focus.
rationale: The centered modal is a heavy interaction pattern that obscures context, blocks the underlying page, and forces an additional dismissal step. Applying it universally reduces the pattern's meaning when genuine confirmation is needed.
violations: Every overlay interaction uses the same centered card with dark backdrop
Close X icon positioned top-right without label
Title row even when content is self-explanatory
Backdrop opacity consistently ~50% black regardless of modal content
controls: Side slide-in panel for detail views that reference parent content
Inline expansion or accordion for supplementary information
Confirmation inline within the triggering element
Non-modal dialog for quick actions that don't need exclusive focus
borderlineCases: Destructive actions requiring confirmation before proceeding
contextualExceptions: Complex forms that genuinely need exclusive user focus
detectorSignal: A centered card element with a dark semi-transparent overlay covering the page background, containing a close button and action buttons.
matcherProposal: Detect a positioned overlay with a semi-transparent background containing a centered rounded card with a close button and action buttons at the bottom.
detectorLimitations: Cannot assess if the modal is the appropriate pattern for the specific interaction
falsePositiveScenarios: Confirmations and flows that genuinely need exclusive focus
mitigationStrategy: Flag with context about alternative overlay patterns that might better preserve user context.

fixturePlans.violation: Centered modal for viewing item details: dark backdrop, close X, title, content, action buttons bottom-right.
fixturePlans.control: Side slide-in panel for viewing item details: keeps parent page visible, preserves scroll position.
fixturePlans.borderline: Delete confirmation modal where the exclusive focus is intentional and necessary.

cleanRoomAttestations: Developed without access to proprietary prompt collections.
overlapNotes: Not covered by existing rules. Name parallels layout.centered-everything but addresses modal overlays specifically.
